SS2_ITEM_NKOTASHERITAGE_LORE:
"Mi amici. Ego congregavi vos omnes hodie ad communicandum aliquas importantes novas.

Primum, dolet cor meum adnuntiare quod Nkota, ultima magistrorum nostrorum, mitis matriarcha nostra, infeliciter ex hac vita transivit. Quantum scimus, extremum spiritum pacifice in requie ultima nocte duxit, plena annorum et cum omni negotio hic perfecto. Cum hoc dicto, nullus erit qui eam in ductu succedat. Illa erat ultima lineae suae, nullis viventibus fratribus, sororibus, aut liberis.

Potius, in testamento suo finali, sapientia eius creavit novum principium. Pro nobis. Pro omnibus nostris. Votum eius est ut reliqui huius communitatis dispergantur, aut simul aut separate, in mundum. Fortasse drastica optio est, sed quam ego, personaliter, quoque credo esse optimam huic communitati.

Nkota sepelietur ad destinationem nostram finalem, sicut voluntas eius est. Vos omnes potestis lugere eam et hanc communitatem per tempus, sed nolite morari. Turma peregrinorum iter faciet mecum ad viam magnam griseam, quinquaginta milia passuum occidentem, post hebdomadam. Potestis sequi, aut ire via vestra propria. Vestra electio prorsus est. Si non creditis vos facere iter posse, nolite sollicitari. Nkota fortasse liberos non habuit, ita, sed hereditas eius in spiritu est, non in sanguine. Vos omnes liberi eius estis, et illa proteget vos donec assequamini finem vestrum.

Gratias vobis omnibus pro tempore vestro. Suaderem vos incipere sarcinas vestras pro itinere praeparare."

 - Eulogium pro Nkota et Vico Dago

Septuaginta seconds. She took a moment to breathe. What had once been harsh winds erat no still air, contained intra the miniature biosphere.

Sexaginta seconds. She counted her bullets.

Quadraginta seconds. She counted their heads.

Triginta seconds. The lizards gave her shield a wide berth. Aut they were frightened of this new technology, aut... they were waiting her out.

Viginti seconds. She loaded her shotgun.

SS2_ITEM_SKATEBOARD_LORE:
Wheels, rolling contra unhewn stone. Unrefined, uncoordinated technique- irresponsible et careless in aequalis measure.

"You are doing it wrong."

"I am doing this sicut I please! This est ars, you know, Brother."

"You've failed countless times iam."

"We'll see."

Focused eyes, darting sicut stars reflecting the sky. Lightning flashes contra the remote landscape in the distance. Twinkling thunderbolts catching a stray eye. A single lapse of focus, tunc an impact. A frustrated scoff. Glares, mutual et infuriated, sharp satis to cut per the gap in the air preceding oncoming thunder.

"I'm beginning to habere doubts, Brother."

"..well, I've yet to give up! How could I have failed?"

Impact, skidding quick satis to sear contra the kicked-up dust. Shaky limbs, rising ex the ground. Defiance. Potential energy, mounting into heat et purpose, tangible in the air. Suffocating tension, louder quam thunder. It all comes crashing down.

"Another failure."

"..you know, I don't believe I've failed once, Brother. There's something to be said of ars de the simple act of learning."

"You HAVE failed! Each et every time you have tried you have failed, et I have attempted to teach you- sed you do not listen. I grow tired of teaching et tired of watching!"

Wheels incipiunt to roll contra stone again. They hum et kick sicut an organ attuned to the pulse of the Earth underfoot, barely holding on.

"You incessantly add your own additions to etiam my simplest instructions.."

Forward movement. The thrill of velocity in one's chest. Of feeling your blood et senses rush back, jostled ab inertia. A reminder of truly being alive.

"When you're done playing cum all this, sine me to demonstrate what I've tried to teach."

Uncontrollable energy, wild et frenzied- forces converging together tandem. A perfect storm.
Rotatio, freed of an axis et speed- ille essential rush of heat et velocity, mounting in his chest, becoming the only thing quod matters. Wind roaring in his ears- the perfect symphony of chaos. One single instantaneous collision of gears et fate, wheels et stone- et, tandem, perfection. Accomplished.

"...quod erat better."

"Isn't it?"

Four eyes meet, et pro the first time in a long while, both tell the truth.

"...You did it, Brother."

Tandem, the wheels stop.
